name: India
description: Geography, history, culture, and landmarks of the world's most populous country.

questions.q: What is the capital of India?
Which ocean borders India to the south?
Which mountain range forms India's northern border?

source.notes: Includes Mount Everest, K2, and Kanchenjunga (the highest peak entirely in India).

questions.q: Which river, considered sacred by Hindus, is the longest entirely in India?

source.notes: About 2,525 km long. (The Brahmaputra is longer but most of its course is outside India.)

questions.q: What is the national animal of India?

source.notes: The national bird is the peacock; the national animal is the Bengal tiger.

questions.q: In which Indian city is the Taj Mahal located?

source.notes: On the south bank of the Yamuna River.

questions.q: Which Mughal emperor commissioned the Taj Mahal as a mausoleum for his wife?

source.notes: Shah Jahan commissioned it for Mumtaz Mahal. Completed around 1648.

questions.q: Which Indian city is nicknamed the 'Pink City'?

source.notes: Capital of Rajasthan; the old city was painted pink in 1876 to welcome the Prince of Wales.

questions.q: Which Indian city is known as 'India's Silicon Valley'?
Which desert lies in the west of India?

source.notes: Mostly in Rajasthan, extends into Pakistan.

questions.q: In what year did India gain independence from British rule?

source.notes: August 15, 1947. The same day Pakistan was created (its Independence Day is August 14).

questions.q: Who led India's non-violent independence movement and is honored as 'Father of the Nation'?
Who served as the first Prime Minister of India?
Who was India's first (and so far only) female Prime Minister?

source.notes: Daughter of Jawaharlal Nehru. Served two terms; assassinated in 1984.

questions.q: Who was the principal architect of the Indian Constitution?

source.notes: Bhimrao Ramji Ambedkar; chair of the Drafting Committee. The Constitution took effect January 26, 1950 (Republic Day).

questions.q: Which Indian festival, often called the 'Festival of Lights', celebrates good over evil?

source.notes: Diwali (Deepavali) typically falls in October or November.

questions.q: Which spring festival is known as the 'Festival of Colors' in India?

source.notes: Holi celebrates the arrival of spring with colored powders and water.

questions.q: What is the official currency of India?
Which Indian city is the heart of the Bollywood film industry?

source.notes: The name 'Bollywood' is a portmanteau of 'Bombay' (Mumbai's former name) and 'Hollywood'.

questions.q: Which large central plateau is the dominant geographical feature of southern India?

source.notes: Bounded by the Western and Eastern Ghats.
